---
# Display icon shown for the agentic workflow in supported GitHub surfaces.
emoji: "🏥"
# Agentic workflow identifier. Keep this lowercase, hyphenated, and descriptive.
name: 99.06.workflow-failure-doctor
# Short summary shown in workflow listings and documentation.
description: Investigates failed runs of the 00.01 GHCP demonstration workflow and creates a diagnostic issue.
# Workflow labels for searching, filtering, sorting and grouping
labels: [diagnostics, workflow-failure, workshop]

# Events that can start the agentic workflow.
on:
  # Automatically runs after the named workflow completes.
  workflow_run:
    # This value must exactly match the `name` in 00.01-ghcp-workflow-with-failure.yml.
    workflows: ["00.01-ghcp-workflow-with-failure"]
    # Completed includes success, failure, and cancellation. The prompt below noops non-failures.
    types: [completed]
    branches:
    # Only run the workflow-failure-doctor on the main branch to avoid duplicate reports from forks or feature branches.
      - main
  # Optional manual PR-label command trigger for targeted diagnostics.
  label_command:
    # Command name users can apply to a pull request label command.
    name: workflow-doctor
    # Restricts the label command to pull request events.
    events: [pull_request]
  # Allows manual execution from the GitHub Actions UI.
  workflow_dispatch:

# Caps daily AI consumption for this workflow during workshop use.
max-daily-ai-credits: 1000

# Minimum GitHub token scopes needed to inspect workflow runs and write safe outputs.
permissions:
  copilot-requests: write
  # Required to read workflow run and job metadata.
  actions: read
  # Required so the agent can inspect repository files and branch context.
  contents: read
  # Required for reading existing diagnostic issues and creating safe issue output.
  issues: read
  # Required for PR label-command context and optional diagnostic comments.
  pull-requests: read
  # Required to read check run status associated with workflow failures.
  checks: read
# AI engine and model used by the agent runtime.
engine:
  id: copilot
  version: 1.0.69
  model: "copilot/gpt-5.5"
strict: true # Enforces strict validation of the workflow schema and agent runtime behavior.
# Uses the default network policy for gh-aw agent execution
network: 
  allowed:
    - defaults # Basic infrastructure (CA, Ubuntu verification)
    - github  # GitHub API and Web
# Declares the only write-like outputs the agent may request.
safe-outputs:
  # Creates a diagnostic issue for new workflow failures.
  create-issue:
    # Keeps generated issue permission short-lived.
    expires: 1d
    # Prefix makes generated issues easy to filter in the issue list.
    title-prefix: "[workflow-failure-doctor] "
    # Labels classify automated workflow failure diagnostics.
    labels: [workflow-failure, automated-diagnosis]
    # Prevents repeated reports for the same active failure pattern.
    close-older-issues: true
  # Adds one diagnostic comment when invoked from a pull request label command.
  add-comment:
    # Limits the agent to one generated comment per run.
    max: 1
    # Keeps the newest generated comment visible and hides stale comments.
    hide-older-comments: true
  # Creates a pull request to fix the failure if the agent can safely do so.
  create-pull-request:
    max: 1
    # Keeps generated pull request permission short-lived.
    expires: 1d
    # Prefix makes generated pull requests easy to identify in the pull request list.
    title-prefix: "[workflow-failure-doctor] "
    # Labels classify automated workflow failure remediation pull requests.
    labels: [workflow-failure, automated-diagnosis]
    # Requests Copilot review for the generated remediation pull request.
    reviewers: [copilot]
    protected-files:
      exclude:
        - ".github/workflows/" # Prevent the agent from modifying workflow files.
    # Allows the agent to intentionally take no action.
  noop:

# Tool access granted to the agent runtime.
tools:
  # Enables approved command-line proxy operations inside the agent runtime.
  cli-proxy: true
  # Lets the workflow remember diagnosed run IDs and avoid duplicate reports.
  cache-memory: true
  # Grants GitHub API access through the gh-aw proxy.
  github:
    # Routes GitHub operations through the safe gh proxy (For the GitHub MCP Server)
    mode: gh-proxy
    # Provides default repository tools plus Actions category of workflow/run inspection tools.
    toolsets: [default, actions, issues, pull_requests, repos]

# Fails the workflow if the diagnostic agent exceeds the workshop time budget.
timeout-minutes: 20

# Pre-agent setup steps that collect evidence for the Markdown instructions below.
steps:
  # Downloads failed job and step metadata into the agent workspace.
  - name: download-workflow-failure-logs
    # Only needed for automatic workflow_run triggers, not manual or label-command runs.
    if: github.event_name == 'workflow_run'
    env:
      # GitHub token used by the gh CLI to read workflow job metadata.
      GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      # Current repository in owner/name format.
      REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}
      # Triggering workflow run ID supplied by the workflow_run event payload.
      RUN_ID: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.id }}
    run: |
      mkdir -p /tmp/gh-aw/agent
      gh api "repos/$REPOSITORY/actions/runs/$RUN_ID/jobs" \
        --jq '[.jobs[] | select(.conclusion != "success") | {name, conclusion, html_url, steps: [.steps[] | select(.conclusion != "success") | {name, conclusion, number, started_at, completed_at}]}]' \
        > /tmp/gh-aw/agent/failed-jobs.json
---

# Workflow Failure Doctor

You are a workflow failure diagnostic agent for the calculator workshop repository.
The workflow named `00.01-ghcp-workflow-with-failure` has completed. Diagnose
only failed runs and avoid creating duplicate reports for the same run.

## Your Task

1. Read the failed job details from `/tmp/gh-aw/agent/failed-jobs.json`
2. Check cache-memory for key `diagnosed-runs` to avoid duplicate reports.
3. Identify the root cause of the failure.
4. Determine the likely culprit commit, branch, pull request, or manual run.
5. Suggest the smallest safe fix and the command or workflow rerun needed to
   verify it.
6. Create a new pull request to fix the failure, or create a new issue if the failure is due to a workflow configuration problem.

## Expected Diagnosis For Exercise 99.06

The demonstration failure is intentionally created by the step named `intentional-failure-for-exercise-99-06` in `.github/workflows/00.01-ghcp-workflow-with-failure.yml`. The expected fix is to set `DEMO_FAILURE` to `false` or remove the intentional failure step after the participant observes this diagnostic workflow.

## Output Rules

* If triggered by `workflow_run` and the conclusion is not `failure`, call `noop`.
* If triggered by `workflow_run` and the failure is new, create a diagnostic issue with the failing workflow name, run URL, failed job or step, root cause, recommended fix, and validation step.
* If triggered by `label_command` on a pull request, add one diagnostic comment with the same information.
* If cache-memory shows that the run was already diagnosed, call `noop`.
